originalTitle: 'Speed, money and compassion' - lessons from an Ebola survivor and other experts
title: Smart Ways to Beat Germs: Learning from Ebola
content:
Hello, super kids of Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I'm here to tell you a very important story. It's a story about learning from the past so we can all stay safe and healthy today. We're going to talk about something called Ebola. Now, don't worry! This isn't a scary story. It's a story about how brave people helped each other and how we got super smart at keeping everyone well. We learned so much from a sickness that happened a long, long time ago – almost ten years! Imagine, you were probably just a tiny baby then, or maybe not even born yet! Our story today will show how we use old lessons to be super safe now.

What is Ebola?

Ebola is like a very tiny germ, so tiny you can't see it with your eyes. You need a special microscope to see these little germs. These germs can make people very sick. But here's the good news: Ebola germs don't fly in the air like some other sicknesses, like a cough or a cold. You can't catch it just by standing next to someone. You get it if you touch the body fluids of a sick person. What are body fluids? Things like sweat, or blood, or if someone throws up. That's why we always tell you to wash your hands often! Washing your hands with soap and clean water helps get rid of all kinds of tiny germs, including ones that make us sick. Did you know? The sickness is named after a river in Africa, called the Ebola River. That's how it got its name! When someone gets Ebola, they might feel very tired, have a fever, or a stomach ache. It's a serious sickness, but doctors and nurses are super heroes who know how to help.

Learning from a Big Sickness Ago

A long, long time ago, almost ten years ago now, many people in some parts of West Africa got very sick with Ebola. It was a very big problem, and many families were sad. It was tough because people didn't know enough about the sickness, and it spread quickly in some places. But even though it was a hard time, people were very brave. Doctors, nurses, and even ordinary people helped each other. They learned super important lessons that help us stay safe today. It's like when you make a mistake playing a game, you learn how to play better next time, right? Well, grown-ups learned big lessons about how to fight sickness better.

Our Super Teachers: Survivors and Smart Helpers

Who helped us learn? Two special groups of people! First, there are the "survivors." These are people who got sick with Ebola but then got all better! They are very strong and brave. They shared their stories and helped everyone understand what it was like. Their stories taught us a lot about how to care for people who are sick. Second, there are "smart helpers" or "experts." These are doctors, scientists, and health workers who know a lot about sicknesses. They worked super hard to find ways to stop the sickness from spreading and to help people get well. Together, they taught us three super important lessons: speed, money, and compassion. Did you know? When you get better from Ebola, your body becomes super smart! It learns how to fight off those tiny germs again if they ever try to come back.

Lesson 1: Be Super Fast! (Speed)

Imagine a tiny fire starting. If you put it out super fast, it won't spread and become a big fire, right? It's the same with sickness! The first lesson is to be super, super fast. When someone feels sick, it's important to tell a grown-up right away. Then, doctors and nurses can help them quickly. Being fast means we can find sick people, help them feel better, and stop the germs from spreading to other people. The faster we act, the fewer people get sick. It's like a superhero team working together as quickly as possible to save the day!

Lesson 2: We Need Help and Tools! (Money)

Our second lesson is about needing "money." Now, money isn't just for toys or sweets! Money helps us buy important things to fight sickness. It helps us buy special gloves and masks for doctors and nurses to wear so they don't get sick. It helps us buy medicines to make sick people feel better. Money also helps build safe places where sick people can get special care. It helps buy soap and clean water for everyone to wash their hands. So, when countries and people give money, they are helping to protect everyone and get all the right tools to fight those tiny germs. Did you know? Special suits that doctors wear to help sick people are called Personal Protective Equipment, or PPE for short! They are like superhero costumes that protect them.

Lesson 3: Be Kind and Caring! (Compassion)

The third and final super lesson is about being kind and caring. We call this "compassion." When someone is sick, they might feel scared and lonely. It's super important to be kind to them and their families. We should never blame people for getting sick. Sickness can happen to anyone. Instead, we show them love and support. We help them get the care they need and make sure they feel loved. Being kind helps sick people feel better in their hearts, and it helps everyone work together to fight the sickness. It reminds us that we are all one big family in Africa.

How We Stay Safe Today

Because we learned so much, we are much better at staying safe now. Here are some super tips for you: * **Wash your hands:** Always wash your hands with soap and clean water, especially before eating and after using the toilet. This washes away those tiny germs! * **Tell a grown-up:** If you feel sick or see someone who looks very sick, tell a grown-up like your mom, dad, or teacher right away. They know what to do! * **Don't touch:** Try not to touch sick people, especially their body fluids. Doctors and nurses wear special gear to do this safely. * **Listen to health workers:** Always listen to doctors, nurses, and health workers. They are here to help us all stay healthy and safe!

Our Future is Bright!

So, you see, kids! Even though something big and tough happened a long time ago, we learned amazing lessons. We are smarter, faster, and kinder than ever before in fighting sickness. We have brave doctors, smart scientists, and caring communities. We are ready to help each other and keep our beautiful continent of Africa healthy and strong! Remember, washing hands is your superpower! Stay safe, super kids!
summary: Super kids of Africa, we're learning important lessons from brave people who faced Ebola a long time ago, so we can stay smart, fast, and kind in keeping everyone healthy today!
whyMatters: It matters because learning from the past helps us be ready for any sickness, protect our friends and family, and keep everyone in Africa safe and strong.

originalTitle: Putin vows retaliation after accusing Ukraine of hitting student dormitory
title: Let's Talk About Peace: News from Far, Far Away
content:
Hello, Super Kids!

Hello, amazing super kids from all over Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here. Today, we have an important story to share with you. It's about two countries that are very far away from us. They are called Russia and Ukraine. We always hope for peace in the world. Peace means everyone is happy and safe. It means no fighting. But sometimes, big countries have big problems.

What's Happening Far Away?

Imagine two friends who used to play together. One day, they start to argue a lot. This is like a very, very big argument, but between two whole countries! It's happening far away in a part of the world called Europe. These two countries, Russia and Ukraine, have been having a difficult time. They have been disagreeing for a long time. People all over the world wish for them to find peace. They hope the big disagreement can stop.

Two Big Countries: Russia and Ukraine

Russia is a very, very big country. It's so big, it covers a huge part of the world map! Ukraine is its neighbour. They share a border, just like you might share a fence with your neighbour. Did you know? Russia is the largest country in the world! It's bigger than some whole continents! Ukraine is much smaller than Russia, but it's still a big country too.

What is a "Drone Unit"?

Now, let's talk about something called a "drone unit." Imagine a toy airplane that can fly without a person sitting inside it. That's like a drone! Drones can be small, or they can be very big. People use drones for many things, like taking cool pictures from the sky or even delivering small packages. A "drone unit" is a special group of people who use these drones for their country's army. They use special drones for specific tasks.

What Happened Last Week?

Last week, something important happened between Russia and Ukraine. The country of Ukraine said that its army used drones. They said their special drones hit a special "drone unit" of the Russian army. This happened in a place that Russia controls right now. It was a place where the Russian army had their special drones. Ukraine said it was a military place, meaning it was for the army.

Why Are Leaders Upset?

When one army says it hit another army's special unit, the leaders of the countries get very, very upset. The leader of Russia, who is called Mr. Putin, heard this news. Mr. Putin was very angry. He said that Ukraine had also hit a building where students live! A "dormitory" is like a big house where many students sleep and live while they study. Ukraine did not say it hit a student dormitory. Ukraine said it hit a military unit. So there is a disagreement about what was hit. Did you know? Leaders of countries are like the captains of a very big team. They make important decisions for everyone in their country.

What Does "Retaliation" Mean?

Mr. Putin also said something called "retaliation." That's a big word! "Retaliation" means doing something back to someone who did something to you. It's like if a friend accidentally steps on your foot, and you then decide to step on theirs. But this is on a very, very big scale, between whole countries. So, Mr. Putin said Russia would do something back to Ukraine because he was very upset about what happened.

Why is This Important for Us?

You might think, "Why is this important for us in Africa, so far away?" Well, when big countries have problems, it can affect the whole world. Everything is connected! It can make things like food or petrol (which your parents might use for their car) more expensive for everyone. It can also make people feel worried and not safe. Everyone wants the world to be a safe and happy place. We want children everywhere to grow up in peace and go to school and play without fear. Did you know? Africa is a continent with many, many beautiful countries, different languages, and amazing animals! We are all connected to the rest of the world.

Wishing for Peace

No one wants to see fighting. Everyone hopes that Russia and Ukraine can stop their big disagreement. We hope they can find a way to talk and be peaceful again. Peace is when everyone feels safe and happy. It's when people can live together without hurting each other. It's what we wish for all the children in the world, everywhere. Let's all hope for peace to come to these countries very soon. Our hearts are with all the children there.

Let's Learn More!

Did you know? "Dormitory" is a fancy word for a big sleeping house, often for students or workers. It comes from an old word meaning "to sleep." Did you know? Ukraine is famous for its beautiful sunflower fields! Sunflowers are very tall and always turn their heads to follow the sun across the sky. Did you know? Russia has a very famous church with colourful onion-shaped domes called St. Basil's Cathedral. It looks like something from a fairy tale! We hope that leaders can talk things out, just like good friends learn to do after an argument. Let's keep wishing for a bright and peaceful future for everyone. Thank you for listening, super kids!
summary: Far away, Russia and Ukraine are having a big disagreement after Ukraine said its army hit a Russian army unit, making Russia's leader upset and promise to respond.
whyMatters: When countries have big disagreements, it can affect everyone in the world, making us wish for peace and safety for all children.

originalTitle: 'Unselfish' over 'ego' - what do leaks say about Tuchel's England?
title: Oops! England's Big Football Secret Got Out Early!
content:
Jambo, my friends! That means hello in Swahili! I’m your friendly news reporter, bringing you exciting stories from all over. Today, we have a very important story about football! You know, that amazing game where players kick a ball and everyone cheers?

What is the World Cup?

Imagine the biggest football party in the whole wide world! That’s what the World Cup is. Teams from different countries play against each other to find out who is the best. It only happens once every four years, so it's very special! **Did you know?** The World Cup is so big, people all over the world watch it! It makes everyone feel excited and happy. To play in the World Cup, each country needs a super team. These teams have the best players from their country. Choosing these players is a very, very big deal.

Choosing the Special Team

Before the World Cup starts, the coach of each team must pick a special group of players. This group is called the 'squad'. Imagine you are having a birthday party and you need to choose your special friends to invite. It’s a bit like that! The coach thinks very carefully. He watches many games. He chooses players who are strong, fast, and good at working together. It’s a very hard job to pick only a few from so many good players. Once the coach has picked his team, it’s a BIG secret. Why is it a secret? Because everyone wants to be surprised! It’s exciting to wait and hear the names of the chosen heroes all at once.

A Secret Was Told Too Soon!

Now, here’s where our story gets a little bit tricky, but don't worry, we'll make it simple. In England, a country far away, the coach was getting ready to tell everyone his special World Cup team. It was going to be a big announcement on TV and the radio. But something unexpected happened! Before the coach could say the names, someone told the secret. Oops! The list of players was known by many people *before* it was supposed to be. Imagine someone telling you what your birthday present is before you even open it! That would take away some of the fun, wouldn't it? This is what happened with England's football team. The secret got out. This is called a 'leak'. It means information that was supposed to be private got shared publicly too early.

How Did Everyone Feel?

When the secret got out early, some people felt a bit sad. The coach probably felt disappointed. He wanted to share the news in a special way. The players who were chosen might have felt happy, but maybe a little less surprised. And the players who weren't chosen might have heard the news in a not-so-nice way, instead of hearing it from their coach directly. It’s always better to hear big news directly from the person who is supposed to tell you, right? It shows respect and care. **Did you know?** Football players train very, very hard for many years to get a chance to play in the World Cup! It's a dream come true for them.

Why Keeping Secrets is Important

This whole story makes us think about why it’s important to keep secrets, especially big ones. When we keep a secret, it shows that we respect other people. We respect their plans and their feelings. It shows we are thinking about the group, not just ourselves. Imagine if everyone always told secrets! There would be no fun surprises. Important things might get messed up. It's like playing a game where everyone tries to cheat; it just spoils the fun for everyone.

Thinking About the Team

When this big secret came out, many wise people started talking. They talked about 'team spirit'. What is team spirit? It means everyone on the team works together, helps each other, and thinks about what is best for the *whole* team. They also talked about 'thinking only about yourself'. Sometimes, people might want to be the first to share something, or they might not think about how their actions affect others. This is not good team spirit. For a football team, team spirit is super important. If players don't trust each other, or if someone wants to be more important than the team, they might not play well together. So, this story teaches us to always think about the team. Think about others. Be kind and respectful. Don't spoil surprises.

Looking Forward to Fair Play

The World Cup is all about fair play and exciting games. It's about countries coming together through sport. Even though this secret came out early, the England team will still play their best. And we hope that everyone learns that big news should be shared at the right time and in the right way. It’s a lesson for all of us, big and small. Let’s always try to be good teammates in everything we do! Let’s cheer for fair play and big, wonderful surprises! **Did you know?** There are 11 players on a football team when they play a match. They all have different jobs to help the team win! That’s all for today from your news reporter! Keep smiling and keep cheering for your favorite teams! Bye for now!
summary: England's football team had its special World Cup squad list leaked before it was announced, making people think about the importance of keeping secrets and team spirit.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us that keeping secrets and thinking about others, especially in a team, is very important for fair play and respect.
